title: Understanding Zero-Knowledge Proofs in Arcium
summary: A concise cryptography-oriented explanation of how proof systems fit into the broader confidentiality story.

bodySections.title: Proofs in Context
bodySections.body: Zero-knowledge language appears in the Arcium conversation because readers need a way to separate private execution from blind trust.

bodySections.title: What proofs do well
bodySections.body: Proof systems help establish confidence in claims about hidden computation or hidden state. They make it easier to verify outcomes without revealing everything that produced them.

bodySections.title: What this article is not saying
bodySections.body: This guide is not arguing that every privacy problem is a zero-knowledge problem. The point is to show how proof language fits into the wider Arcium story instead of replacing the need to understand MPC and infrastructure.
